introSection: Desperado is an Arrow Dynamics steel hyper roller coaster located at Buffalo Bill's Resort & Casino in Primm, Nevada, USA. It is currently standing but not operating with no known plans to reopen.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Desperado opened on August 19, 1994, and was the third hyper coaster to ever be built.

In 1999, magician Lance Burton performed an escape act on the roller coaster. Burton was chained to the roller coaster track and escaped before the train would have hit him.

The last time Buffalo Bill's stated the coaster was open to the public was August 11, 2019. The attractions page on the hotel's website listed the coaster as "currently closed until further notice" starting December 2019 or earlier.

On December 14, 2019, the ride was inspected by Clark County, Nevada, and put out of service as it was deemed unsafe according to a notice placed on the ride's operation panel. The notice was still there in December 2022.

According to the Las Vegas Weekly, in a February 2020 phone call with a hotel employee, it was said that Desperado only had a "slight chance" of ever reopening, however no offical comment was given by the hotel on the coaster's status. Buffalo Bill's closed temporarly, and later indefenetly due to the COVID-19 pandemic on March 18, 2020.

The hotel reopened on December 23, 2022, but the coaster remained closed. In February 2023, Buffalo Bill's General Manager Jerry West stated that Desperado had been cycled once monthly for the duration of its closure to keep it operational, but that it didn't have a reopening date.

In mid-2023, the coaster was removed from the hotel's website. It also had operating hours listed on an April 2023 hotel directory, but there's no mention of it opening during this time. As of November 2024, all of the coaster's signage has been removed, and its entrance is blocked by arcade games.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride's supports were designed by Ride Centerline.

fullText:
Desperado is an Arrow Dynamics steel hyper roller coaster located at Buffalo Bill's Resort & Casino in Primm, Nevada, USA. It is currently standing but not operating with no known plans to reopen.

Desperado opened on August 19, 1994, and was the third hyper coaster to ever be built.

In 1999, magician Lance Burton performed an escape act on the roller coaster. Burton was chained to the roller coaster track and escaped before the train would have hit him.

The last time Buffalo Bill's stated the coaster was open to the public was August 11, 2019. The attractions page on the hotel's website listed the coaster as "currently closed until further notice" starting December 2019 or earlier.

On December 14, 2019, the ride was inspected by Clark County, Nevada, and put out of service as it was deemed unsafe according to a notice placed on the ride's operation panel. The notice was still there in December 2022.

According to the Las Vegas Weekly, in a February 2020 phone call with a hotel employee, it was said that Desperado only had a "slight chance" of ever reopening, however no offical comment was given by the hotel on the coaster's status. Buffalo Bill's closed temporarly, and later indefenetly due to the COVID-19 pandemic on March 18, 2020.

The hotel reopened on December 23, 2022, but the coaster remained closed. In February 2023, Buffalo Bill's General Manager Jerry West stated that Desperado had been cycled once monthly for the duration of its closure to keep it operational, but that it didn't have a reopening date.

In mid-2023, the coaster was removed from the hotel's website. It also had operating hours listed on an April 2023 hotel directory, but there's no mention of it opening during this time. As of November 2024, all of the coaster's signage has been removed, and its entrance is blocked by arcade games.

The ride's supports were designed by Ride Centerline.

Yellow track and beige supports.
